title: McMillan Township, Ontonagon County, Michigan

* [Agate](/wiki/Agate,_Michigan "Agate, Michigan") * [Algonquin](/wiki/Algonquin,_Michigan "Algonquin, Michigan") * [Calderwood](/wiki/Calderwood,_Michigan "Calderwood, Michigan") * [Carp Lake](/wiki/Carp_Lake_Township,_Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Carp Lake Township, Ontonagon County, Michigan") * [Craigsmere](/wiki/Craigsmere,_Michigan "Craigsmere, Michigan") * [Maple Grove](/wiki/Maple_Grove,_Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Maple Grove, Ontonagon County, Michigan") * [Matchwood](/wiki/Matchwood,_Michigan "Matchwood, Michigan") * [Paulding](/wiki/Paulding,_Michigan "Paulding, Michigan") * [Paynesville](/wiki/Paynesville,_Michigan "Paynesville, Michigan") * [Robbins](/wiki/Robbins,_Michigan "Robbins, Michigan") * [Trout Creek](/wiki/Trout_Creek,_Michigan "Trout Creek, Michigan") * [Victoria](/wiki/Victoria,_Michigan "Victoria, Michigan")

is a [civil township](/wiki/Civil_township "Civil township") of [Ontonagon County](/wiki/Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Ontonagon County, Michigan") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). The population was 406 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area") and [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") at [46°32′07″N 89°16′52″W﻿ / ﻿46.53528°N 89.28111°W﻿ / 46.53528; -89.28111](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Ontonagon_County,_Michigan&params=46_32_07_N_89_16_52_W_), near where [M-28](/wiki/M-28_(Michigan_highway) "M-28 (Michigan highway)") crosses the south branch of the [Ontonagon River](/wiki/Ontonagon_River "Ontonagon River").[[4]](#cite_note-4) The community began as a logging camp in 1888 and in 1889 gained a depot named **Ewen Station** on the [Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railway](/wiki/Duluth,_South_Shore_and_Atlantic_Railway "Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railway") (now the [Soo Line Railroad](/wiki/Soo_Line_Railroad "Soo Line Railroad")). It was named for W. A. Ewen, a treasurer of the railroad. It also received a Post Office named Ewen Station in 1889, shortened to Ewen in 1894.[[5]](#cite_note-Romig-5) The Ewen Post Office, [ZIP code](/wiki/ZIP_code "ZIP code") 49925, serves much of the township area. [Bruce Crossing](/wiki/Bruce_Crossing,_Michigan "Bruce Crossing, Michigan") is about 5 miles east on M-28 and [Matchwood](/wiki/Matchwood,_Michigan "Matchwood, Michigan") about 6 miles west.
["U.S. Census website"](https://www.census.gov). [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"). Retrieved January 31, 2008.

[U.S. Geological Survey Geographic Names Information System: Ewen, Michigan](https://edits.nationalmap.gov/apps/gaz-domestic/public/search/names/1619879)

**McMillan Township** is a [civil township](/wiki/Civil_township "Civil township") of [Ontonagon County](/wiki/Ontonagon_County,_Michigan "Ontonagon County, Michigan") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). The population was 406 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=McMillan_Township,_Ontonagon_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the township has a total area of 70.50 square miles (182.59 km2), of which 70.43 square miles (182.41 km2) is land and 0.07 square miles (0.18 km2) (0.10%) is water.

### Communities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=McMillan_Township,_Ontonagon_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Communities")]

* **[Ewen](/wiki/Ewen,_Michigan "Ewen, Michigan")** is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area") and [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") at [46°32′07″N 89°16′52″W﻿ / ﻿46.53528°N 89.28111°W﻿ / 46.53528; -89.28111](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Ontonagon_County,_Michigan&params=46_32_07_N_89_16_52_W_), near where [M-28](/wiki/M-28_(Michigan_highway) "M-28 (Michigan highway)") crosses the south branch of the [Ontonagon River](/wiki/Ontonagon_River "Ontonagon River").[[4]](#cite_note-4) The community began as a logging camp in 1888 and in 1889 gained a depot named **Ewen Station** on the [Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railway](/wiki/Duluth,_South_Shore_and_Atlantic_Railway "Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railway") (now the [Soo Line Railroad](/wiki/Soo_Line_Railroad "Soo Line Railroad")). It was named for W. A. Ewen, a treasurer of the railroad. It also received a Post Office named Ewen Station in 1889, shortened to Ewen in 1894.[[5]](#cite_note-Romig-5) The Ewen Post Office, [ZIP code](/wiki/ZIP_code "ZIP code") 49925, serves much of the township area. [Bruce Crossing](/wiki/Bruce_Crossing,_Michigan "Bruce Crossing, Michigan") is about 5 miles east on M-28 and [Matchwood](/wiki/Matchwood,_Michigan "Matchwood, Michigan") about 6 miles west.
